summary: node-fetch-utils pulls mutable GitHub master-branch tarball as mandatory dependency
details: package.json declares a mandatory runtime dependency 'node-fetch-core' resolved from https://github.com/Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz — an unpinned reference to the master branch HEAD with no commit SHA, tag, or version constraint. Every `npm install node-fetch-utils` fetches whatever is currently on that branch, including any preinstall/install/postinstall lifecycle hooks present in the fetched tarball. The maintainer (or anyone who compromises the maintainer's GitHub account) can swap the tarball contents at any time without publishing a new npm version, so npm registry scanners see a clean package while installers receive arbitrary mutable code. The dependency is not imported anywhere in this package's own source, which is inconsistent with a legitimate runtime requirement and consistent with a smuggling channel for delivering install-time code through a clean-looking npm shell. The harm fires automatically on default install via npm's normal dependency resolution and lifecycle execution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
